content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
November 5,
1992
Russell Kiessig
P.O.
Box 115
Avila Beach, California
93424
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
G—92-613
Dear Mr. Kiessig:
We have received your letter regarding the validity of your
absentee ballot because of an erroneous middle initial.
The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to
interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act.
1
The Political
Reform Act was established for several purposes,
some of which are
disclosure of sources of campaign contributions, disclosure of
assets and income of public officials, disqualification of public
officials from decisions which could affect their private
financial interests,
and regulation of the activities of lobbyists
to prevent improper influences on public officials.
The advice you request in your letter is not covered by the
Political Reform Act; therefore,
we cannot advise you on this
matter.
We suggest that you continue to communicate with the
Election Division of your County Clerk-Recorder’s Office until the
matter is resolved to your satisfaction.
Sincerely,
-
: t
DeDe Lind
Secretary
Fair Political Practices Commission
:dl
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
q.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660

sections.question: Validity of absentee ballot due to an erroneous middle initial
sections.conclusion: The advice you request in your letter is not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on this matter. We suggest that you continue to communicate with the Election Division of your County Clerk-Recorder's Office until the matter is resolved to your satisfaction.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether an absentee ballot is valid when submitted with an erroneous middle initial on the voter's registration information.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the matter falls outside the scope of the Political Reform Act, which governs campaign finance disclosure, official financial interests, and lobbyist regulation, not ballot validity issues.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter where FPPC explicitly states the matter is outside their jurisdiction under the Political Reform Act. No statutory analysis or political reform principles are applied.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Validity of absentee ballot due to an erroneous middle initial

CONCLUSION: The advice you request in your letter is not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on this matter. We suggest that you continue to communicate with the Election Division of your County Clerk-Recorder's Office until the matter is resolved to your satisfaction.

Box 115 Avila Beach, California 93424 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. G—92-613 Dear Mr. Kiessig: We have received your letter regarding the validity of your absentee ballot because of an erroneous middle initial. The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act. 1 The Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions, disclosure of assets and income of public officials, disqualification of public officials from decisions which could affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on public officials. The advice you request in your letter is not covered by the Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on this matter. embedding.summary: Russell Kiessig requested FPPC advice on whether his absentee ballot was valid despite an erroneous middle initial, but the FPPC declined because ballot validity is not covered by the Political Reform Act and directed him to contact his County Clerk-Recorder's Office Election Division.
